# Transcrição: Don't_take_things_you_love_for_granted

- **Fonte:** Don't_take_things_you_love_for_granted
- **Duração:** 01:03
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:03]

I have a computer in my studio that I use. It's a little laptop and that little computer has been just the champion. It's been definitely for the last five to six years, reliable. Every time I come in, I press it, it works. I didn't put it in the super easy way, but I don't know what the time. It's always got to work. Two weeks ago, in the middle of an important online presentation, it just died. I didn't realize how important that laptop was to me and should have died. That's just a little metaphor for the things that are incredibly reliable in our lives, the things that are extremely consistent, are the things we take advantage of. The relationships that we forget to invest in the most are the ones that are closest to us, whether it's you and Chantel or me and Pei Wen. That is the easiest relationship to take for granted. However, when things go bad, it's the one relationship we depend on the most. It's only like that analogy of, you know, you have to keep cleaning the windows because you're doing a lot of kilometers together. Yeah. Right. And that's what happens with these long term relationships. Yeah, just be more sensitive to the things in life that are so important to you. Because of consistency, daily convenience, because of daily reliance, we become blind to its importance.
